content:
Observations We observe galaxies across cosmic time. ALMA JWST Molecular lines High-redshift galaxies Ziyi
Guo Galactic Chemical Evolution I connect observations and theory to understand how galaxies form stars and build up their elements across cosmic time. Research Publications CV Theory We build models to interpret and understand. Galactic chemical evolution Stellar yields Initial mass function Star-formation histories Research Highlights CO isotopologues as IMF tracers Using ¹³CO/C¹⁸O to probe IMF variations in high-redshift galaxies.
Learn more → Galactic chemical evolution Building physically motivated GCE models that connect stellar yields, SFH, gas flows, and abundance patterns.
Learn more → From local galaxies to the early Universe Combining ALMA and JWST observations with models to study star formation across cosmic time.
Learn more → Research Themes 1. Observations ALMA and JWST measurements of molecular lines and chemical abundances.
2. Chemical fingerprints Isotopes and elements record the physical conditions of stellar populations.
3. GCE modeling Chemical evolution models connect yields, SFH, inflow, outflow, and abundances.
4. IMF & yields Constraining the IMF and stellar nucleosynthesis from massive stars to low metallicity.
5. Galaxy evolution Understanding how galaxies build up their mass and elements over cosmic time.
“The elements we observe today are the memory of galaxies.
My goal is to read that memory.”
